findings.description: Hierarchy/application ratio rule (primary>secondary>tertiary dominance, blues/golds dominant, tertiary as accents) is not represented by any candidate rule; this is a distinct governance statement about color hierarchy that should be its own rule.

payload.description: Apply palette per primary>secondary>tertiary dominance ratio; blues/golds dominate, tertiary colors are accents/surfaces only

findings.description: Headline/heading color rule and body text color rule (with background-conditional variants) are not captured by any candidate rule despite being clearly specified color_application statements, comparable in specificity to the included link-color rule.

payload.description: Headlines: Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient); Body text: Graphite #212121 on light, White on dark

findings.description: Chart/data-visualization color rule, including the strict reservation of Sunshine/Gold for emphasizing key figures (a hard_constraint-level statement given 'strictly reserved' language), has no corresponding rule in the cluster.

payload.description: Charts: Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures only; gray for placebo

findings.description: Coral usage restriction (never for primary CTAs/headlines/data, plus GENERAL guidance to avoid in email unless brief specifies) is a distinct multi-part rule missing from the cluster; it also carries an explicit conditional exception that would need governance capture if added.

payload.description: Coral is sparing tertiary accent only; never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data; avoid in email sections unless brief calls for it

governance.exception: unless the brief calls for it

findings.description: Source unit explicitly tags this rule '[GENERAL]', signaling a governance scope/applicability marker distinguishing it from channel- or context-specific rules; the candidate rule gives no indication of this governance facet.

Callout surface tint specification is phrased descriptively (like the omitted headline/body-text lines) rather than with imperative/must language, yet it is assigned polarity=must and hardness=strong_default while structurally similar statements (u0002, u0003) were excluded entirely — inconsistent treatment across similarly-worded units.
Confirmed correct: source uses explicit 'must be maintained in all instances' language, consistent with hard_constraint/must classification; no error found, listed for completeness.
